examples: Ethnic Composition: a mixture of Sinhalese, Dravidian, Arab and Black.
There has also been reciprocal influence between Sanskrit and the Dravidian languages.
South Indian speakers of Dravidian languages considered that a cultural imposition from the north.
These displaced people acquired a distinct name -- "Dravidian" -- derived from a Sanskrit word for people living south of the Vindhya.
